title: Safety Boundary and LOTO Walkthrough
summary: This scene places the learner in an energized work area where PPE selection, approach boundaries, lockout/tagout, and test-before-touch decisions determine whether the task can proceed safely.
learningObjectives: Identify shock, arc flash, blast, and fire hazards in a work area.
Apply PPE and boundary rules before approaching energized equipment.
Follow a correct apprentice-level lockout/tagout verification sequence.

environment.setting: Mechanical and electrical room with panelboard, hazard zones, and worker staging area
environment.focus: Safe approach behavior before hands-on electrical work

interactables.label: PPE rack
interactables.teaches: Matches gloves, hard hat, face shield, and footwear to the task risk.

interactables.label: Safety zone rings
interactables.teaches: Shows green, yellow, and red approach zones around energized equipment.

interactables.label: Disconnect and lockout point
interactables.teaches: Walks the learner through isolate, lock, tag, release stored energy, and verify.

tasks: Dress the worker with task-appropriate PPE.
Stay outside the wrong boundary and explain why it is unsafe to continue.
Complete the LOTO sequence in the correct order.
Prove absence of voltage using the live-dead-live method.
completion: Learner chooses correct PPE and boundary controls.
Learner completes LOTO without skipped steps.
Learner verifies zero-energy state before work begins.
